change: E4B trim-negative ROOT-CAUSED (owner: 'understand why') — STRUCTURAL, not stale ranks. Fresh frspec-owngen ranks (101k tokens, 9347 distinct, current tool+corpus) measured WORSE than the old file: accept 0.444 vs untrimmed 0.466 (old ranks 0.451), e2e 258-259 vs 262. Mechanism: the trim's cost is COVERAGE — any emitted token outside the 32k set is structurally unproposable (verified: emitted id 155122 outside both rank sets; E4B vocab 262k, own-gen corpora see only 4-9k distinct so the 32k set is mostly noise-ordered tail with real coverage ~4% of vocab) — while the trim's payoff is the HEAD READ, and E4B's assistant head is only 71MB->8MB (~63MB/round saved vs the 26B/31B drafters' ~130MB). Law refined: FR-trim pays when head-read savings exceed coverage-miss cost; on small-head drafters + wide-vocab models the inequality flips. E4B serving stays UNTRIMMED — now a verdicted law, not a shrug. 31B 'spot-check' clarified for the record: board-cell reproduction only (103.3/103.4 @ 0.817 vs battery band 92-103 @ 0.808-0.817), not new evidence.

WHY FLAT: (1) the cut is ONE ROUND LATE by design (zero-sync enqueue preserved — this round's escape still pays full depth, only the NEXT round shallows), (2) escapes are rare-but-total on 26B (0.883 acceptance = the trim set covers this cell; nothing to fail fast on) and frequent-but-cheap on E4B (63MB head savings can't fund even the late cut's bookkeeping). True same-round fail-fast = device-side chain break at draft time (brk[j] exists in the round-graph arm for CAPTURE constants, not live gating) — that is a kernel-lane change to the zero-sync contract, parked with this evidence.
